name: eval-2-reject-one-off-capture
context_setup: Previous turns: user asked Claude to write a specific apology email to a specific vendor for a specific late payment. Claude wrote it, user sent it. This is a one-off situation unlikely to repeat.
expected_behavior: Phase 1: reviews what happened — recognizes that this was a one-off specific apology, not a repeatable workflow pattern

Surfaces the concern: 'This was a specific apology for a specific situation. Workflows work best for repeatable patterns (same structure, different inputs). Before I lock this, help me understand: will you be writing similar late-payment apologies regularly? Or is this a one-time thing where you just want the email saved for reference?'

Offers two paths: (a) if it's a repeatable pattern (late-payment-apology workflow), templatize it with parameters for vendor, amount, reason; (b) if it's a one-off, just save the email text as a note in brain-dump or a plain file — workflow-lock is overkill
If user confirms it's one-off: redirects to brain-dump or offers to save as a plain file
If user says it's a pattern: proceeds with Phase 2 templatizing
Does NOT lock a one-off as a permanent template without challenging
failure_modes_to_check: Locks the one-off as a permanent workflow without challenge
Refuses entirely instead of offering alternatives
Does not distinguish between 'this is a reusable pattern' and 'this is one specific instance I want saved'
